rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KUEI-SHI LEI
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Supplement filed 8/10/23 states that petitioner is asking the court to determine the 50% of interest in the real property subject to this petition passing to the surviving spouse's trust; however, this petition does not allow said bypass. Petition may need to seek "passing to the surviving spouse's estate" not to his Trust. the surviving spouse's estate. 2) Proposed Order not submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Way Pung To Comply With Subpoena Duces Tecum And Pay Petitioner Her Expenses For The Motion
rulings.Rulings: Tentative ruling by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Mary Bong's Motion for Order Directing Way Pung to Comply with Subpoena Duces Tecum and Pay Petitioner for Expenses of the Moton is GRANTED. Mr. Pung has had several months to produce documents responsive to the subpoena and has failed to comply with his obligations. The only ground Mr. Pung advances to deny the motion is his assertion that he is now working to resolve the issues raised in this motion. The Court declines to accept Mr. Pung's assertion as a valid legal excuse for his failure to respond properly to the subpoena. Since being served with the subpoena in February 2023, Mr. Pung has claimed more than once that he has fully responded to the subpoena by producing all responsive documents. It is clear from the email communications between Petitioner's counsel and Mr. Pung's counsel that this is not the case. Given the present set of circumstances, an order compelling Mr. Pung to comply with his duties under the terms of the subpoena is warranted, as are sanctions. Mr. Pung has demonstrated a continued lack of good faith in searching for and producing documents responsive to the subpoena. Petitioner has offered reasonable accommodations, providing Mr. Pung an extension of 60 days from the original production date of March 1, 2023 to May 1, 2023, to produce documents. After producing only 11 documents (all in Microsoft Word's native file format) Mr. Pung chose not to respond to any meet and confer communications made by Mr. Bassi. The only response found in this record from Mr. Pung was on May 28, 2023, wherein Mr. Pung asked for "two weeks so I can search for more," thereby contradicting his earlier Declaration of Custodian of Records that "All of the records called for in the subpoena ... in [his] custody ... [had] been released." It was only after Petitioner filed and served this motion that Mr. Pung decided to act. Mr. Pung's retention of counsel does not help his cause. Aside from an email on June 9, 2023 making initial contact with Petitioner's counsel, Mr. Pung's counsel Mr. Rueppel made no further contact for the next month and half. Notably, Mr. Pung's counsel did not respond to the earlier meet and confer communications sent to Mr. Pung. Nor does it appear that there was any intention of providing a supplemental production to Petitioner. In an email dated July 3, 2023, Mr. Rueppel states: "Since they have filed a motion to compel, we don't need to do a supplemental production yet." The lack of good faith effort at informal resolution, first by Mr. Pung and then his attorney, warrants the granting of the instant motion, including sanctions. Mr. Pung's attempt to excuse his evasive discovery response, including on account of his "very minimal knowledge of litigation practice," is unpersuasive. Mr. Pung has been licensed as an attorney in the state of California for over 20 years. As an attorney, Mr. Pung is presumed to know how to look up the law if he had questions about the subpoena. Indeed, the subpoena form itself contains statutory references and states in bold capital letters: "Disobedience of this subpoena may be punished as contempt by this court." It is farfetched to believe that, as an attorney, Mr. Pung was not aware of his responsibility to respond to a subpoena fully and completely. Continued to line 2 of 2

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Way Pung To Comply With Subpoena Duces Tecum And Pay Petitioner Her Expenses For The Motion (Added 2nd Line For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Continued from line 1 of 2 For the foregoing reasons, Petitioner's motion is granted. Mr. Pung is ordered to produce all documents responsive to the subpoena to Respondent's counsel of record, Brian T. Hafter or Kevin Rodriguez, for their review pursuant to the Stipulation Regarding Production, within 10 days of this order. Mr. Pung is further ordered to make available all wet-ink original signed documents for inspection, examination, and testing, and to produce either copies of requested line-item entries of his notary journals or acknowledge that no such line-item entries exist. The Court has reviewed the request for attorney's fees carefully. The hours spent preparing the instant motion appear reasonable, as do the hourly billing rates. Mr. Pung is ordered to pay Petitioner's reasonable attorney's fees and costs in the total amount of $17,940.00. (C.C.P. 2025.480(j), 2023.030(a).) Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. End of Tentative Ruling.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA GRIFFIS
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared. (1) Need a declaration of due diligent search of the decedent's heirs at law. Petition merely states that decedent was not survived by no blood relatives. (2) Duties and Liabilities [Form DE 147] not on file. (3) Confidential Statement of Driver's License and Birth Date [Form DE-147S not on file. LRSF 14.18.E. (4) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. (5) If surviving spouse is the only successor in interest, if administration is needed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SUSAN MARIKO KOBAYASHI AND CHARLES SUNGWON HAN REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED APRIL 29, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification And Amendment Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/27/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) The trust date included in the petition caption and case title is incorrect. An ex parte petition must be filed for an order authorizing the clerk to correct the case title. 3) Is the trust real property community property or either settlor's separate property? Please provide facts in support of the property characterization. 4) Is there any trust asset other than the real property? If so, please provide a brief description, value, and property characterization of each asset. 5) Is the date of birth of each child of the settlors stated in Article 1.4 of the trust true and correct? 6) Have both children of the settlors consented to the proposed elimination of the Bypass Trust? 7) Have Ann Kobayashi and Dale Kobayashi named in Article 7.4 of the trust consented to the proposed designation of the settlors' children as successor trustees of the Bypass Trust? It should be noted that the petitioner's power to designate successor trustees does not apply to the Bypass Trust under Article 7.3. 8) The petition must allege circumstances not known to and anticipated by the deceased settlor re her designation of successor trustees of the Bypass Trust in support of the modification under Probate Code section 15409. 9) Each Article of the trust sought to be modified must be set forth verbatim in the petition and proposed order. 10) Each Article of the trust sought to be deleted in its entirety must be identified as such in the petition and proposed order. An appearance may be required on 10/11/2023 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.
